content.text:
Question:
                                How many days ago did I read the March 15th issue of The New Yorker?

                                Question date:
                                2023/04/01 (Sat) 16:41

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                12 days ago. 13 days (including the last day) is also acceptable.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:gpt4_7bc6cf22
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has read about climate change in the March 15 issue of The New Yorker.; scope=User media consumption and topic interests from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="In a past chat on 2023-03-20, the user asked for reputable sources to learn more about climate change after reading a New Yorker article."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I finally got around to reading the March 15th issue of The New Yorker today and there was a fascinating article on it."
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-15 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user enjoys The New York Times daily briefing for quick rundowns of top news stories.; scope=User news-consumption preferences from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user mentioned The New York Times daily briefing while discussing staying informed and news organizations' use of AI."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I've been enjoying The New York Times' daily briefing, which gives me a quick rundown of the top news stories."
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-16 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has been following The New York Times coverage of recent elections in the user's state.; scope=User election-news following from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user cited the Times' in-depth reporting on recent state elections while discussing its daily briefing and AI in journalism."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I've been following their coverage of the recent elections in my state"
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-17 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to learn how news organizations like The New York Times use AI in reporting and news gathering.; scope=User learning goals around AI and journalism from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user asked this after mentioning the Times' daily briefing and coverage of recent state elections."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="Can you tell me more about how news organizations like The New York Times are using AI in their reporting and news gathering processes?"
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-21 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user enjoys The Daily from The New York Times.; scope=User podcast preferences from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user cited The Daily and Pod Save America as podcasts they enjoy when asking for additional podcast recommendations."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I've been enjoying podcasts like The Daily from The New York Times"
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-12 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has read about artificial intelligence in Time Magazine.; scope=User technology-topic exposure from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user introduced interest in AI by saying they read a fascinating Time Magazine article about it the previous week."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I read a fascinating article about it on Time Magazine last week."
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-8 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has read about US-China trade tensions in The Economist.; scope=User topic exposure from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user asked for more current information on US-China trade tensions after reading an Economist article about it the previous month."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I read an article about it in The Economist last month"
- claim hot-path-25ab60a456d4-claim-22 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user enjoys Pod Save America.; scope=User podcast preferences from imported LongMemEval session 1.; context="The user cited The Daily and Pod Save America as podcasts they enjoy when asking for additional podcast recommendations."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="and Pod Save America, which offer in-depth discussions and analysis on current events and politics."

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-25ab60a456d4-note-2 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: User's information-seeking pattern for news, climate, trade, AI, and podcasts; The user tends to deepen interest after reading or listening to reputable mainstream sources, then asks for more authoritative or in-depth follow-up sources. Examples include climate change after The New Yorker, US-China trade after The Economist, AI after Time Magazine, AI in journalism after The New York Times daily briefing, and podcast recommendations after enjoying The Daily and Pod Save America.; scope=User's media consumption and follow-up research behavior in the 2023-03-20 LongMemEval session; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I'd like to know more about the current situation."
- note hot-path-25ab60a456d4-note-3 [DIRECTION/ACTIVE]: People Magazine cancellation rationale and replacement direction; The user was leaning toward canceling People Magazine because they no longer have time to read it regularly and can get most relevant news online for free, while still valuing occasional celebrity interviews. The intended replacement direction was to explore online celebrity news/interviews and podcast-based current-events analysis instead of maintaining the print subscription.; scope=User's magazine subscription and entertainment-news consumption direction as of 2023-03-20; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 quote="I think I'll cancel my subscription to People Magazine and explore other ways to stay informed about celebrity news and interviews."

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:677c8d4f2bcbee61c29ac55acd6e9587 [external_record ref=longmemeval:gpt4_7bc6cf22:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/03/20 (Mon) 01:20
Transcript:
...Session date: 2023/03/20 (Mon) 01:20
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some information on climate change. I finally got around to reading the March 15th issue of The New Yorker today and there was a fascinating article on it. Can you recommend some reputable sources for learning more about the topic?
assistant: The New Yorker article must have sparked your interest in climate change! There are many reputable sources where you can learn more about this critical topic. Here are some trustworthy sources to get you started:...
...3. **InsideClimate News**: A nonprofit news organization dedicated to covering climate change, energy, and environmental issues....
...user: I didn't know there were so many sources available. I've been trying to keep up with The Economist's coverage of global news and politics, but I think I'll start following some of these sources for more in-depth information on climate change. Do you think I could get some information on the trade tensions between the US and China? I read an article about it in The Economist last month, but I'd like to know more about the current situation....
...user: I didn't know the trade tensions were so complex. I think I'll start following Bloomberg Economics and the Peterson Institute for International Economics to stay updated on the situation. I've also been meaning to explore more about artificial intelligence, I read a fascinating article about it on Time Magazine last week. Can you tell me more about the current advancements in AI?...
...assistant: The New York Times' daily briefing is an excellent way to stay informed about current events. The Times is indeed a pioneer in using AI and machine learning to enhance their reporting and news gathering processes. Here's an overview of how they, and other news organizations, are leveraging AI:...
...3. **Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR)**: AI-powered VR and AR experiences can immerse readers in stories, providing a more immersive and empathetic understanding of complex issues....
...2. **bots.nytimes.com**: The Times has developed a range of AI-powered bots that provide readers with personalized news updates, election results, and other information....
...user: I'll definitely keep an eye on how news organizations are using AI to enhance their reporting and news gathering processes. Speaking of reporting, I've been meaning to cancel my subscription to People Magazine, which I got as a gift from my sister last year. I don't really have time to read it anymore, and most of the news I care about I can get online for free. But I have to admit, sometimes I'll flip through it when I'm waiting in line or at the doctor's office. I remember reading an issue last month that had a really interesting interview with a celebrity I like.
assistant: People Magazine can be a fun read, and it's great that you enjoy flipping through it when you have a few minutes to spare. The celebrity interview you mentioned might have been one of the reasons why you've been holding onto the subscription. However, if you're not reading it regularly, it might make sense to cancel the subscription and allocate that money towards something else you enjoy....
...user: I think I'll cancel my subscription to People Magazine and explore other ways to stay informed about celebrity news and interviews. I've been enjoying podcasts like The Daily from The New York Times and Pod Save America, which offer in-depth discussions and analysis on current events and politics. Do you think I could get some recommendations for other podcasts that might be of interest to me?...
...1. **The Watch**: A weekly podcast discussing TV, movies, and pop culture, hosted by The New Yorker's Emily Nussbaum and The Ringer's Chris Ryan....
...1. **Radiolab**: A podcast exploring big questions and ideas through storytelling and conversation....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Climate change; Concept named Climate change.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: US-China trade tensions; Concept named US-China trade tensions.
- entity entity:<2> [ORGANIZATION]: The Economist; Organization named The Economist.
- entity entity:<3> [ORGANIZATION]: The New Yorker; Organization named The New Yorker.
- entity entity:<4> [ORGANIZATION]: Time Magazine; Organization named Time Magazine.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some information on climate change. I finally got around to reading the March 15th issue of The New Yorker today and there was a fascinating article on it. Can you recommend some reputable sources for learning more about the topic?
assistant: The New Yorker article must have sparked your interest in climate change! There are many reputable sources where you can learn more about this critical topic. Here are some trustworthy sources to get you started:...
...3. **InsideClimate News**: A nonprofit news organization dedicated to covering climate change, energy, and environmental issues....
...user: I didn't know there were so many sources available. I've been trying to keep up with The Economist's coverage of global news and politics, but I think I'll start following some of these sources for more in-depth information on climate change. Do you think I could get some information on the trade tensions between the US and China? I read an article about it in The Economist last month, but I'd like to know more about the current situation....
...user: I didn't know the trade tensions were so complex. I think I'll start following Bloomberg Economics and the Peterson Institute for International Economics to stay updated on the situation. I've also been meaning to explore more about artificial intelligence, I read a fascinating article about it on Time Magazine last week. Can you tell me more about the current advancements in AI?...
...assistant: The New York Times' daily briefing is an excellent way to stay informed about current events. The Times is indeed a pioneer in using AI and machine learning to enhance their reporting and news gathering processes. Here's an overview of how they, and other news organizations, are leveraging AI:...
...3. **Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR)**: AI-powered VR and AR experiences can immerse readers in stories, providing a more immersive and empathetic understanding of complex issues....
...2. **bots.nytimes.com**: The Times has developed a range of AI-powered bots that provide readers with personalized news updates, election results, and other information....
...user: I'll definitely keep an eye on how news organizations are using AI to enhance their reporting and news gathering processes. Speaking of reporting, I've been meaning to cancel my subscription to People Magazine, which I got as a gift from my sister last year. I don't really have time to read it anymore, and most of the news I care about I can get online for free. But I have to admit, sometimes I'll flip through it when I'm waiting in line or at the doctor's office. I remember reading an issue last month that had a really interesting interview with a celebrity I like.
assistant: People Magazine can be a fun read, and it's great that you enjoy flipping through it when you have a few minutes to spare. The celebrity interview you mentioned might have been one of the reasons why you've been holding onto the subscription. However, if you're not reading it regularly, it might make sense to cancel the subscription and allocate that money towards something else you enjoy....
...user: I think I'll cancel my subscription to People Magazine and explore other ways to stay informed about celebrity news and interviews. I've been enjoying podcasts like The Daily from The New York Times and Pod Save America, which offer in-depth discussions and analysis on current events and politics. Do you think I could get some recommendations for other podcasts that might be of interest to me?...
...1. **The Watch**: A weekly podcast discussing TV, movies, and pop culture, hosted by The New Yorker's Emily Nussbaum and The Ringer's Chris Ryan....
...1. **Radiolab**: A podcast exploring big questions and ideas through storytelling and conversation....

structured.fullText: {"hypothesis":"The user read it on 2023/03/20, which was 12 days before 2023/04/01.","reason":"The retrieved transcript is dated 2023/03/20 and says the user read the March 15 issue “today,” allowing the 12-day calculation.","supported":true}
